description: SEGA's flagship platformer franchise starring the world's fastest hedgehog. Launched in 1991 as SEGA's answer to Mario, Sonic became a cultural icon spanning games, cartoons, comics, films, and one of gaming's most complex and contested canons.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog
media.description: The original. Green Hill Zone, loop-de-loops, and BLAST PROCESSING. Sonic races through six zones to stop Dr. Robotnik.
media.notes: Core: genesis_plus_gx. The genesis classic — start here.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog (Game Gear)
media.description: Scaled-down Game Gear port of the original — entirely different zone layout and level design.
media.notes: Core: genesis_plus_gx (GG mode). Separate game, not just a port.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 2 (Game Gear)
media.description: Tails' first appearance as a playable character, before the Genesis sequel. Handheld-original zones.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 2
media.description: Introduces Miles 'Tails' Prower and the Spin Dash. Bigger, faster, and more polished than the original.

media.title: Sonic CD
media.description: Sega CD exclusive. Time travel mechanic — visit Past, Present, and Good/Bad Futures in each zone. Introduces Metal Sonic and Amy Rose.
media.notes: Core: genesis_plus_gx (with Sega CD BIOS). Also on modern platforms digitally.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog Spinball
media.description: Pinball game set in Robotnik's Veg-O-Fortress. Mechanically distinct from mainline Sonic.

media.title: Dr. Robotnik's Mean Bean Machine
media.description: Puyo Puyo reskin with Robotnik's cast. The franchise's first puzzle game.

media.title: Sonic Chaos
media.description: First Game Gear Sonic with a fully non-linear world map. Tails is playable.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 3
media.description: Introduces Knuckles. Designed to be played together with Sonic & Knuckles via the lock-on cartridge. First half of a two-part story.
media.notes: Core: genesis_plus_gx. Play with S&K locked on ideally.

media.title: Sonic & Knuckles
media.description: Second half of the Sonic 3 story. Lock-on technology lets you attach Sonic 2 or Sonic 3 for expanded content.
media.notes: Core: genesis_plus_gx. Lock-on Sonic 3 for the full S3&K experience.

media.title: Sonic Triple Trouble
media.description: Nack the Weasel's first appearance. Handheld-original adventure across six zones.

media.title: Sonic Drift
media.description: Japan-exclusive kart racer on Game Gear. First racing game in the franchise.

media.title: Knuckles' Chaotix
media.description: 32X exclusive. Features the Chaotix team (Espio, Vector, Charmy) long before their later reintroduction in Heroes.

media.title: Sonic the Fighters
media.description: 3D arcade fighting game. Full cast of Sonic characters slugging it out.
media.notes: Available on Steam. Arcade original via MAME.

media.title: Sonic 3D Blast
media.description: Isometric pseudo-3D platformer. Collect Flickies across multiple worlds to face Robotnik.
media.notes: Core: genesis_plus_gx (GEN) or Mednafen Saturn. Optional.

media.title: Sonic Blast
media.description: Last Game Gear Sonic. Pre-rendered 3D sprites, five zones.

media.title: Sonic R
media.description: On-foot racing game with an infamous licensed soundtrack. Cult following.
media.notes: Mednafen Saturn or PC version on Steam.

media.title: Sonic Adventure
media.description: First fully 3D mainline Sonic. Six playable characters, hub world, Chao Garden. Introduces Chaos and Perfect Chaos.
media.notes: DX remaster on PC (Steam). Core: flycast for Dreamcast original.

media.title: Sonic Pocket Adventure
media.description: Neo Geo Pocket Color exclusive. Portable 2D adventure with remixed Sonic 2 zones.

media.title: Sonic Shuffle
media.description: Mario Party-style board game for Dreamcast. Mixed reception.

media.title: Sonic Adventure 2
media.description: Introduces Shadow the Hedgehog and Rouge. Hero/Dark split campaign. Famous for the Prison Island escape scene.
media.notes: HD remaster on PC (Steam). Chao Garden compatible with SA1 import.

media.title: Sonic Advance
media.description: First GBA Sonic. Four playable characters, fast 2D gameplay, connects to Sonic Adventure 2 Battle Chao garden via link cable.

media.title: Sonic Advance 2
media.description: Speed-focused sequel. Introduces Cream the Rabbit. Boost-style momentum platforming before it was a thing.

media.title: Sonic Mega Collection
media.description: Compilation of Genesis-era Sonic titles for GameCube.
media.notes: Core: dolphin. Skip in favour of individual ROMs.

media.title: Sonic Battle
media.description: GBA arena fighter. Emerl the Gizoid builds a moveset by absorbing others' techniques.
media.notes: Core: mgba. Connects to SA2 Battle.

media.title: Sonic Heroes
media.description: Team-based gameplay, four teams of three each tackling the same story. Reintroduces the Chaotix.

media.title: Sonic Advance 3
media.description: Tag-team mechanic. Each partner pair has unique abilities. Last of the GBA Advance trilogy.

media.title: Shadow the Hedgehog
media.description: Shadow's standalone game with guns and multiple branching endings across 22 possible levels. Infamously edgy.
media.notes: Core: dolphin. Optional — but plot-relevant for Shadow's arc.

media.title: Sonic Rush
media.description: Introduces Blaze the Cat. Dual-screen speed platforming, boost mechanic's true debut.

media.title: Sonic Riders
media.description: Hoverboard racing. Introduces the Babylon Rogues. Air resource management gives it a unique rhythm.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog (2006)
media.description: The 'Sonic 06' reboot attempt. Three campaigns: Sonic, Shadow, Silver. Infamous for bugs and loading times. The most divisive mainline entry.
media.notes: No PC port. Xbox360 emulation via xenia. PS3 via RPCS3. Optional but historically significant.

media.title: Sonic and the Secret Rings
media.description: Arabian Nights setting. On-rails motion controls. First Storybook series entry.

media.title: Sonic Rush Adventure
media.description: Sequel to Sonic Rush. Marine the Raccoon joins. Nautical exploration between zones.

media.title: Sonic Riders: Zero Gravity
media.description: Sequel to Sonic Riders. Gravity-manipulation mechanic replaces air resource system.

media.title: Sonic Chronicles: The Dark Brotherhood
media.description: Turn-based RPG by BioWare. Introduces the Marauders and Imperator Ix. Legally complicated due to Ken Penders' lawsuit.
media.notes: Core: melonds. Legally inaccessible digitally — physical only.

media.title: Sonic Unleashed
media.description: Day/Night split: fast daytime Sonic, slow werehog brawler at night. Daytime stages are widely beloved.
media.notes: Core: dolphin (Wii/PS2). PS3 via RPCS3, 360 via xenia.

media.title: Sonic and the Black Knight
media.description: Arthurian legends setting. Sword combat via Wiimote waggle. Second Storybook series entry.

media.title: Sonic Colors
media.description: Introduces the Wisps. Space theme park run by Robotnik. Widely considered a franchise high point for the modern era.
media.notes: Core: dolphin (Wii). HD remaster (Colors Ultimate) on PC/Switch.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 4: Episode I
media.description: Episodic 2D sequel to the classic trilogy. Mixed reception — physics don't match the originals.
media.notes: PC via Steam (delisted). Mobile versions delisted.

media.title: Sonic Free Riders
media.description: Kinect-only Riders sequel. Motion control racing.

media.title: Sonic Generations
media.description: Classic Sonic meets Modern Sonic. 25 years of levels reinvented in both styles. Near-universal critical acclaim.
media.notes: PC via Steam (partially delisted). Emulate 360/PS3 versions. 3DS version is separate game.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 4: Episode II
media.description: Metal Sonic returns. Tails co-op. Better physics than Episode I.
media.notes: PC via Steam. Mobile versions delisted.

media.title: Sonic & All-Stars Racing Transformed
media.description: Kart racer across SEGA franchise tracks. Vehicles transform between car, boat, and plane. One of the best kart racers of its era.
media.notes: PC via Steam. Optional but excellent.

media.title: Sonic Lost World
media.description: Cylindrical world platforming. Deadly Six are the villains. Divisive controls.
media.notes: Core: dolphin (Wii U). PC on Steam.

media.title: Sonic Boom: Rise of Lyric
media.description: 3D brawler set in the Sonic Boom alternate universe. Critically panned.
media.notes: Core: dolphin. Skip unless a completionist.

media.title: Sonic Boom: Shattered Crystal
media.description: Handheld companion to Rise of Lyric. Better received.

media.title: Sonic Runners
media.description: Mobile endless runner. Free-to-play. Servers shut down in 2016 — fan revival 'Sonic Runners Revival' exists.

media.title: Sonic Runners Adventure
media.description: Premium mobile endless runner, no microtransactions. One-time purchase, later delisted.

media.title: Sonic Dash
media.description: Temple Run-style endless runner. Free-to-play, still active.

media.title: Sonic Dash 2: Sonic Boom
media.description: Sonic Boom universe endless runner. Multiplayer racing.

media.title: Sonic Jump
media.description: Doodle Jump-style vertical platformer. Classic and modern Sonic.

media.title: Sonic Jump Fever
media.description: Score-attack sequel to Sonic Jump. Delisted from stores.

media.title: Sonic Forces: Speed Battle
media.description: Multiplayer mobile racing game. Still active as of 2026.

media.title: Sonic Mania
media.description: Fan-made love letter turned official release. Remixed classic zones plus original stages. Widely regarded as the best 2D Sonic since the Genesis era.
media.notes: PC via Steam. Mania Plus adds Encore mode and Mighty/Ray.

media.title: Sonic Forces
media.description: Classic Sonic, Modern Sonic, and a custom Avatar character. Robotnik actually wins at the start. Divisive.

media.title: Team Sonic Racing
media.description: Team-mechanics kart racer. Heavy emphasis on cooperative driving within teams of three.

media.title: Sonic at the Olympic Games — Tokyo 2020
media.description: Mobile Olympic minigame collection. Servers shut down December 2021.

media.title: Sonic Colors: Ultimate
media.description: HD remaster of Sonic Colors with new Rival Rush mode and additional cosmetics.

media.title: Sonic Origins
media.description: Compilation of Sonic 1, 2, CD, and 3&K with new Anniversary modes and animated story cutscenes.
media.notes: PC via Steam. Plus DLC adds Mirror Mode and additional unlockables.

media.title: Sonic Frontiers
media.description: First open-world Sonic game. Cyber Space zones interspersed with vast Starfall Islands. Major franchise tonal shift toward mystery and lore.
media.notes: PC via Steam. Substantial post-launch DLC including Final Horizon.

media.title: Sonic Superstars
media.description: 4-player co-op 2D Sonic with new Emerald Powers replacing classic special stages.

media.title: Sonic x Shadow Generations
media.description: Remaster of Sonic Generations plus new Shadow Generations campaign set after Sonic Forces.
media.notes: PC via Steam. Shadow campaign is new content, not DLC.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog (DiC, SatAM)
media.description: Darker serialised cartoon. Sonic and the Freedom Fighters fight Robotnik's totalitarian empire. Different continuity to the games. Fan-beloved.
media.notes: Known as SatAM. Unrelated to Adventures of Sonic below.

media.title: Adventures of Sonic the Hedgehog
media.description: Comedic slapstick cartoon. Sonic and Tails outwit Dr. Robotnik and his bots Scratch and Grounder weekly.
media.notes: Aired alongside SatAM as a separate, comedic counterpart.

media.title: Sonic Underground
media.description: Sonic leads a royal band — he and his siblings Sonia and Manic play instruments while fighting Robotnik. Musical numbers every episode.
media.notes: No series conclusion — cancelled before reaching its planned ending.

media.title: Sonic X
media.description: Sonic and friends are transported to Earth. Adapts Adventure, Adventure 2, Heroes, and Egg Fleet arcs. Season 3 adapts the Metarex saga.
media.notes: Japanese version (Sonic X JP) preferred — dub censors significant content. 78 episodes including the 26-ep S3 exclusive to Japan.

media.title: Sonic Boom (TV Series)
media.description: Comedy-focused series set in the Boom alternate universe. Self-aware humour, strong character writing. Separate from main game continuity.
media.notes: All episodes on Netflix in some regions. Boom universe.

media.title: Sonic Prime
media.description: Sonic shatters the Paradox Prism and fractures reality into alternate universes. Dark, action-heavy Netflix CG series.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog: The Movie (OVA)
media.description: Japanese OVA in two parts. Sonic and Tails protect the Land of the Sky from Metal Sonic and Dr. Eggman. High production quality.
media.notes: Released in the US in 1999 as a single movie. Sought after.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog (2020)
media.description: Live-action/CG hybrid film. Sonic hides on Earth, befriends Sheriff Tom Wachowski, and faces Jim Carrey's Eggman. Massive box office success after a controversial redesign.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 2 (2022)
media.description: Introduces Knuckles and Tails. Eggman returns with the Master Emerald. Higher stakes, bigger action.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog 3 (2024)
media.description: Shadow the Hedgehog is introduced as an adversary. Based loosely on Shadow's origin in SA2.

media.title: Knuckles (Paramount+ Series)
media.description: Live-action spin-off miniseries. Knuckles trains Wade Whipple as a warrior while navigating Reno, Nevada.
media.notes: Streaming on Paramount+. Set between Sonic Movie 2 and 3.

media.title: Archie Sonic the Hedgehog Comics
media.description: Long-running American comic book series. 290+ issues plus spin-offs. Major original cast including Sally, Bunnie, Antoine, NICOLE. Universe wiped and rebooted in 2013 following Ken Penders lawsuit.
media.notes: 290 main issues, plus Super Specials, Archives, Mega Man crossover. IDW has since replaced it.

media.title: IDW Sonic the Hedgehog Comics
media.description: Current ongoing comic series by IDW Publishing. Picks up after Sonic Forces. Introduces the Metal Virus saga, Surge and Kit, and more. More game-aligned than Archie.
media.notes: Ongoing as of 2026. Collected in trade paperbacks.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog: The Complete Sonic Comic Encyclopedia
media.description: Reference book compiling the entire Archie Sonic universe lore.

media.title: Sonic the Comic (Fleetway)
media.description: UK comic by Fleetway. 223 issues. Features Super Sonic as a separate, evil dark entity. Highly original interpretation.
media.notes: UK publication. Distinct from Archie continuity entirely.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog: Scrapnik Island
media.description: IDW limited series set on a forgotten island of decommissioned robots.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog: Imposter Syndrome
media.description: IDW limited series following Surge and Kit — introduced as dark counterparts to Sonic and Tails.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog: Fang the Hunter
media.description: IDW mini-series spotlighting Nack/Fang the Weasel's return to the IDW continuity.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog: Knuckles' 20th Anniversary Special
media.description: Archie one-shot celebrating Knuckles' anniversary.

media.title: Sonic Mania Adventures
media.description: Official animated web series bridging Sonic Mania and Forces. Wordless slapstick animation in the classic Sonic style. Excellent.
media.notes: Free on SEGA YouTube. 5 episodes + 1 holiday special.

media.title: Team Sonic Racing Overdrive
media.description: Short animated series promoting Team Sonic Racing. Two episodes.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog: Wubstation
media.description: SEGA-produced series of very short animated bumpers set to electronic music. A forgotten gem.

media.title: Sonic Auditorium (Lego Dimensions Shorts)
media.description: Promotional Lego Dimensions web shorts featuring Sonic.

media.title: Sonic Origins Plus Animated Short
media.description: Animated short released with Sonic Origins Plus. Features Amy and Mighty.

media.title: Sonic the Hedgehog: Labyrinth (Archie)
media.description: 4-issue Archie mini-series featuring Knuckles vs a militaristic faction.

media.title: Sonic Mega Drive (Archie)
media.description: Archie comic set in a continuity inspired by the classic games. Part of the post-reboot era.
